offerings.label: Product leadership, at every depth
offerings.heading: The right level of product leadership — for the leap you're making.

items.title: Fractional CPO
items.subtitle: Full ownership of the product function — strategy through execution.
items.body: Scaled to your inflection point: own strategy, align go-to-market, and drive execution — embedded in your team, operating on your cadence, without the cost or ramp of a full-time hire.
triggers: Founder-led product — ready to hand it to a seasoned operator
Scale and complexity have outgrown current product leadership
CPO-caliber experience — without the full-time hire

items.title: Strategic Advisor
items.subtitle: Your team owns the build — I pressure-test direction and sharpen decisions.
items.body: Some bets are too expensive to get wrong. I sharpen product direction, pressure-test the decisions that matter most, and reorient the team around outcomes over output — high-leverage counsel, on your pace, when the stakes are highest.
triggers: You're shipping — but it's not showing up in retention or revenue
A high-stakes bet on the table: raise, new market, or strategic pivot
Strategy is in motion — and direction still feels uncertain

items.title: Executive Operator
items.subtitle: One strategic initiative — I own it from concept to launch.
items.body: What slows launches is rarely the building — it's the unmade decisions: scope, sequencing, positioning, what good looks like. I take ownership of a single bet — an AI-native product, a new product line — making the strategic calls in real time and driving design, build, and iteration to launch.
triggers: A strategic initiative on the table — and no senior owner to drive it
Delivery keeps stalling on decisions only a product executive can make
Deep technical capability that needs translating into a product people actually use

items.title: Craft Coach
items.subtitle: Raise the craft and skills of the leaders and teams already doing the work.
items.body: Your people have the raw material — what compresses the learning curve is working with someone who's done it. Structured 1:1 coaching for your product leader, hands-on team workshops, and AI-native ways of working — building both the leadership and the craft your next stage demands.
triggers: Your product leader is two steps from ready — and you need them ready now
You're betting on AI-native workflows — and no one owns the transition
Velocity is capped by craft and decision-making, not headcount

items.label: ARR growth at Monday.com, scaled past $1B ARR
Churn reduction at BQE Software in under 10 months

about.label: The operator behind the practice
about.heading: Meet Guy Assedou.
about.quote: What drives me is helping leaders <grad>realize their vision</grad> and create real impact

points.title: The inflection point is the job
points.body: The most valuable company moments happen at transition — when the foundation exists but the path forward isn't clear. The work is knowing where to focus and how to make every bet move the metrics that matter.

points.title: Pattern recognition, earned
points.body: Built across B2B SaaS, B2C2C, and consumer — global enterprises and early-stage startups, horizontal and vertical markets. Breadth across distinct contexts — and the reps to master each — is what turns experience into instinct.

points.title: Expertise in the US & Israeli markets
points.body: A decade embedded inside American SaaS, with deep roots in Israel's tech ecosystem — not advising from a distance, but building. GTM dynamics, investor framing, cultural context: I know these markets from the inside.

points.title: Close to the craft
points.body: I mentor PMs and speak at ProductCon LA, ProductWorld, and Product-Led Summit. Staying close to how good product thinking spreads keeps what I bring you sharp.

about.credentials: Kellogg MBA · Technion BS EE · IDF Major

items.q: How is this different from hiring a full-time CPO?
items.a: You get senior product leadership — strategy, execution, and team development — without the six-month search, 12-month ramp, equity dilution, or full-time cost. Engagements are scoped to what you actually need, and can scale up or down as the business evolves. Not sure which model fits? The 30-minute call usually makes it clear.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: It depends on the engagement — and that range is intentional. A focused workshop can be a single day. Advisory retainers run month-to-month with regular touchpoints. Owning a single initiative runs two to four months. Embedded fractional CPO work runs three to six months with a weekly operating cadence. In all cases, you get dedicated time — not a revolving door. The first conversation is usually enough to figure out which shape fits.

Fractional CPO work runs a minimum of three months — strategy needs time to materialize and compound. Advisory retainers run month-to-month. Coaching and workshops can be as short as a single session. The right length becomes clear once we understand what you're solving for.

items.q: How quickly can we get started?
items.a: Typically within 1–2 weeks. The first step is a 30-minute call to understand the situation and see if it's a good fit. If it is, we move fast.

cta.heading: Let's turn your product into leverage.
cta.body: A 30-minute call to map where product leadership creates the most impact. No pitch, no commitment.
